docs.title: JT Lab Notebook
docs.text: Author: Jash Trivedi System: Automated Project Intelligence Tracker

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This system automatically monitors registered project directories and tracks:

  • All new, modified, and deleted files
  • Nextflow pipeline runs (parsed from .nextflow.log and trace files)
  • SLURM job outputs (parsed from slurm-*.out and sacct)
  • Log files (.log, .out, .err)
  • Configuration files and scripts
  • File content hashes for change detection

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Page Purpose Dashboard Statistics and project overview File Registry Complete file inventory per project Pipeline Runs Detected runs with resource usage Annotations File tags and context Notes Manual notes and observations

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: biolab scan -- Scan all projects, detect changes biolab annotate -- Add tags and context to files biolab sync -- Scan + commit + push biolab note -- Add a manual note

docs.title: File Annotations
docs.text:
This page lists all files that have been annotated with tags and context. Use biolab annotate to add annotations, or edit this data in .biolab/annotations.json.

No annotations yet. Use biolab annotate <path> --tags ... --context ... to add context to tracked files.

docs.title: How to Add Context
docs.text:
To annotate files detected by the scanner, use:

biolab annotate --tags "tag1,tag2" --context "description"

Or edit docs/annotations.md directly on GitHub to add tags and context to any tracked file.
